current_medication.name: Rosuvastatin 10mg
current_medication.known_issues: Muscle pain and weakness in some patients; limited LDL reduction at current dose

promotion.how_it_works: Blocks the PCSK9 protein so the liver can remove more LDL cholesterol from the blood
advantages_over_current: Reduces LDL by up to 60% on top of statin therapy
Injected every 2 weeks — no daily pills
No muscle pain side effects common with statins
Proven to reduce cardiovascular events in clinical trials
potential_downsides: Requires self-injection every 2 weeks
May cause injection site reactions
Higher list price without savings program
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may receive Repatha at no cost through the Repatha Ready program
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Patients who have tried and failed statin therapy; income-based qualification

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, interested in learning more
call.agent_notes: Patient has been on statins but still has elevated LDL. Good candidate for add-on therapy.

current_medication.name: Atorvastatin 20mg
current_medication.known_issues: Patient reports occasional muscle cramps; LDL still above target at 145 mg/dL

promotion.how_it_works: A PCSK9 inhibitor that helps the liver clear LDL cholesterol more effectively
advantages_over_current: Can lower LDL by an additional 50-60% beyond statins alone
Self-injection every 2 weeks replaces or supplements daily pills
Lower risk of muscle-related side effects
Dose can be adjusted based on LDL response
potential_downsides: Requires subcutaneous injection
Some patients experience flu-like symptoms initially
Needs refrigerated storage
promotion.benefit_description: Pay as little as $0 per month with the Praluent Copay Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured; must have tried at least one statin

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, has concerns about switching
call.agent_notes: Patient is cautious about new medications. Address muscle pain concerns with current statin.

current_medication.name: Methotrexate 15mg weekly
current_medication.known_issues: Nausea and fatigue after weekly dose; requires regular blood monitoring; taking 8+ weeks to see full effect

promotion.how_it_works: A targeted JAK inhibitor that blocks specific enzymes involved in the inflammatory process
advantages_over_current: Faster onset of action — many patients feel improvement within 2 weeks
Oral daily pill instead of weekly injection or infusion
No routine blood work needed as frequently as methotrexate
Can be used alone or with methotrexate for enhanced effect
potential_downsides: Increased risk of infections — need to monitor
Not recommended for patients with history of blood clots
Higher cost without savings program
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $5 per month with the Rinvoq Complete Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with moderate-to-severe RA who have had inadequate response to methotrexate

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, frustrated with current medication
call.agent_notes: Patient has expressed frustration with methotrexate side effects to her doctor. Likely receptive to alternatives.

current_medication.name: Symbicort 160/4.5 inhaler
current_medication.known_issues: Oral thrush from inhaled corticosteroid; still having 2-3 exacerbations per year; technique-dependent delivery

promotion.how_it_works: A biologic that targets and blocks IL-4 and IL-13 proteins that drive type 2 inflammation in the airways
advantages_over_current: Reduces severe asthma attacks by up to 87% in clinical trials
Injection every 2 weeks — not dependent on inhalation technique
No risk of oral thrush or hoarseness from inhaled steroids
Can reduce or eliminate need for oral corticosteroids
potential_downsides: Self-injection every 2 weeks
Injection site reactions possible
Only for moderate-to-severe type 2 asthma — not a rescue inhaler
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $0 per month with the Dupixent MyWay Copay Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with moderate-to-severe eosinophilic asthma or oral corticosteroid-dependent asthma

call.agent_notes: Patient is analytical and will want specifics about mechanism and clinical data.

current_medication.name: Methotrexate 10mg weekly
current_medication.known_issues: Liver enzyme elevation requiring dose reduction; nausea; hair thinning; 6-month blood monitoring schedule

promotion.how_it_works: Targets and blocks IL-23, a specific protein that drives the overactive immune response causing psoriasis plaques
advantages_over_current: 90% of patients achieved clear or almost clear skin in trials
Only 4 injections per year after initial loading doses
No liver toxicity concerns — no routine blood monitoring needed
No nausea or hair thinning side effects
potential_downsides: Self-injection (though only 4 times per year)
Upper respiratory infections reported in some patients
Must be refrigerated
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $5 per fill with the Skyrizi Complete Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis who are candidates for systemic therapy

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, worried about side effects
call.agent_notes: Patient has had bad experience with current medication side effects. Focus on Skyrizi's safety profile.

current_medication.name: Warfarin 5mg
current_medication.known_issues: Requires weekly INR blood tests; many food and drug interactions; dose adjustments frequent; dietary restrictions with vitamin K foods

promotion.how_it_works: Directly inhibits Factor Xa in the clotting cascade without needing to affect vitamin K metabolism
advantages_over_current: No routine blood monitoring needed — no more weekly INR tests
No dietary restrictions — eat what you want including leafy greens
Fewer drug interactions than warfarin
Lower risk of major bleeding in clinical trials vs warfarin
Fixed dose — no constant adjustments
potential_downsides: No widely available reversal agent for emergencies (though Andexxa exists)
Must be taken twice daily (warfarin is once daily)
Higher list price without copay card
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $10 per month with the Eliquis 360 Support Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients prescribed Eliquis for AFib or DVT/PE

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, tired of blood tests
call.agent_notes: Patient has expressed frustration with frequent INR monitoring. Eliquis eliminates this.

current_medication.name: Flovent HFA 110mcg inhaler
current_medication.known_issues: Requires twice-daily use; oral thrush recurring; voice hoarseness; still using rescue inhaler 3-4 times per week

promotion.how_it_works: Combines three medications in one inhaler: an anti-inflammatory steroid, a long-acting muscarinic antagonist, and a long-acting beta agonist for comprehensive airway control
advantages_over_current: Once-daily dosing vs twice-daily with Flovent
Triple therapy provides better symptom control than ICS alone
Reduces rescue inhaler use significantly
Single device — simpler than carrying multiple inhalers
Built-in dose counter
potential_downsides: May still cause oral thrush (contains ICS component)
Pneumonia risk slightly elevated with ICS use
Not for acute bronchospasm — still need rescue inhaler available
promotion.benefit_description: Pay as little as $0 per month for up to 12 months with the Trelegy savings offer
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with asthma not adequately controlled on ICS alone

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, uses rescue inhaler too often
call.agent_notes: Patient's frequent rescue inhaler use suggests asthma is poorly controlled on current ICS-only therapy.

current_medication.name: Simvastatin 40mg
current_medication.known_issues: Severe muscle pain causing non-adherence; patient has stopped taking medication twice in the past year

promotion.how_it_works: Inhibits cholesterol synthesis in the liver like statins but is activated only in the liver — not in muscles — avoiding muscle-related side effects
advantages_over_current: Works in the liver only — does NOT cause muscle pain
Once-daily oral pill — same convenience as current statin
Can be used alone or added to a lower-dose statin
25% LDL reduction when used alone, up to 38% with low-dose statin
potential_downsides: Less potent LDL reduction than high-dose statins or PCSK9 inhibitors
May increase uric acid levels — not ideal for gout patients
Newer medication with less long-term safety data
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $0 per month with the Nexletol Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients who need additional LDL lowering or cannot tolerate statins

call.agent_notes: Patient has clear statin intolerance with muscle pain. Nexletol's muscle-sparing mechanism is the key selling point.

current_medication.name: Metformin 1000mg twice daily
current_medication.known_issues: Persistent GI side effects (bloating, diarrhea); A1C still at 7.8% above target of 7.0%; weight gain concern

promotion.how_it_works: Mimics the GLP-1 hormone to stimulate insulin release when blood sugar is high, slows gastric emptying, and reduces appetite
advantages_over_current: Superior A1C reduction — typically lowers by 1.5-2% vs metformin's 1-1.5%
Significant weight loss benefit — average 10-15 lbs
Once-weekly injection instead of twice-daily pills
Cardiovascular risk reduction proven in trials
No GI bloating/diarrhea that metformin causes
potential_downsides: Nausea during initial dose titration (usually resolves)
Requires self-injection weekly
Potential risk of pancreatitis (rare)
Not recommended with personal/family history of medullary thyroid cancer
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $25 per month for up to 24 months with the Ozempic Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured Type 2 diabetes patients; not for Type 1 diabetes

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, struggling with blood sugar control
call.agent_notes: Patient's A1C is above target despite metformin. GI side effects affecting adherence. Ozempic addresses both.

current_medication.name: Lisinopril 20mg + Amlodipine 5mg (two pills)
current_medication.known_issues: Persistent dry cough from ACE inhibitor; ankle swelling from amlodipine; taking 2 separate pills daily; BP still 142/88 not at goal

promotion.how_it_works: Combines two mechanisms: blocks angiotensin II receptors (like an ARB) AND inhibits neprilysin to enhance beneficial natriuretic peptides that relax blood vessels
advantages_over_current: No dry cough — uses ARB mechanism instead of ACE inhibitor
Single combination pill replaces two separate medications
Superior blood pressure reduction in clinical studies
Additional heart-protective benefits beyond blood pressure lowering
Less ankle swelling than amlodipine
potential_downsides: Must stop ACE inhibitor 36 hours before starting (washout period)
Can cause dizziness when starting
Higher cost without savings card
Should not be used in pregnancy
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $10 per month with the Entresto Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with hypertension or heart failure who are on or eligible for ARB/ACE therapy

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, bothered by cough and swelling
call.agent_notes: Patient's ACE inhibitor cough and amlodipine ankle swelling are quality-of-life issues. Entresto addresses both.

current_medication.name: Glipizide 10mg twice daily
current_medication.known_issues: Frequent hypoglycemic episodes (low blood sugar); weight gain of 12 lbs since starting; must eat on rigid schedule to avoid crashes

promotion.how_it_works: Blocks the SGLT2 protein in the kidneys, causing excess glucose to be excreted in urine instead of reabsorbed back into the blood
advantages_over_current: Very low risk of hypoglycemia — works independently of insulin
Weight loss of 4-7 lbs on average (vs weight gain with glipizide)
No rigid meal timing required — take once daily any time
Proven cardiovascular and kidney protective benefits
Lowers blood pressure as a bonus effect
potential_downsides: Increased risk of urinary tract and yeast infections
Can cause dehydration — need to drink more water
Rare risk of diabetic ketoacidosis
Not recommended with severe kidney impairment
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $10 per month with the Jardiance Savings Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with Type 2 diabetes

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, frustrated with low blood sugar episodes
call.agent_notes: Patient's hypoglycemia and weight gain from sulfonylurea are significant quality-of-life issues. Jardiance eliminates both.

current_medication.name: Sertraline 150mg (Zoloft)
current_medication.known_issues: Sexual dysfunction; emotional blunting; weight gain of 20 lbs; tried increasing dose but side effects worsened

promotion.how_it_works: Acts on the glutamate NMDA receptor system — a completely different pathway than SSRIs — to rapidly restore neural connections in brain areas affected by depression
advantages_over_current: Rapid onset — many patients feel improvement within hours to days, not weeks
Works through a completely different brain mechanism than SSRIs
No sexual dysfunction or weight gain
Administered in-office twice monthly — not a daily pill
Effective for treatment-resistant depression where SSRIs have failed
potential_downsides: Must be administered in a certified healthcare facility (REMS program)
Requires 2-hour monitoring after each dose
Can cause dissociation, dizziness, and sedation during/after dosing
Twice-monthly office visits required
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $10 per treatment with the Spravato Savings Program
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured adults with treatment-resistant depression or major depressive disorder with suicidal ideation

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, SSRI side effects are unbearable
call.agent_notes: Patient has been on multiple SSRIs with persistent side effects. Spravato's different mechanism and rapid onset are key differentiators.

current_medication.name: Ezetimibe 10mg + Atorvastatin 40mg
current_medication.known_issues: Taking two pills daily; LDL reduced but still at 120 mg/dL (goal <100); mild muscle aches from statin component

promotion.how_it_works: Uses small interfering RNA technology to silence the gene that produces PCSK9 protein in the liver, allowing the liver to clear more LDL cholesterol
advantages_over_current: Only 2 injections per year (given by healthcare provider) after initial doses
Can reduce LDL by an additional 50% on top of current therapy
No daily pills to remember — administered in-office
Novel RNA-based mechanism — first-in-class therapy
No muscle pain side effects
potential_downsides: Requires office visit for injection (though only twice yearly)
Injection site reactions in some patients
Relatively new — approved 2021, less long-term data than statins
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $0 per injection with the Leqvio patient support program
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with ASCVD or familial hypercholesterolemia on maximally tolerated statin therapy

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, interested in reducing pill burden
call.agent_notes: Patient is on two daily cholesterol medications but still not at LDL goal. Leqvio's twice-yearly injection and additional 50% LDL reduction are compelling.

current_medication.name: Topical Betamethasone + Calcipotriol cream
current_medication.known_issues: Only controls mild areas; large body surface area affected (>10%); skin thinning from long-term steroid use; messy application twice daily

promotion.how_it_works: Selectively blocks IL-23, a key cytokine that activates the inflammatory pathway responsible for psoriasis plaque formation
advantages_over_current: Clears skin systemically — treats all affected areas at once, not spot-by-spot
Only 6 injections per year after loading doses
No skin thinning risk like topical steroids
Over 80% of patients achieve 90% skin clearance
No messy creams — simple prefilled auto-injector
potential_downsides: Self-injection required (though only every 8 weeks)
Risk of infections — need to screen for TB before starting
Must be refrigerated
Higher cost without copay card
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $5 per injection with the Tremfya Janssen CarePath Savings Program
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis who are candidates for systemic therapy or phototherapy

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, frustrated with topical treatments
call.agent_notes: Patient has extensive psoriasis poorly controlled with topicals. Systemic biologic therapy is the appropriate next step.

current_medication.name: Methotrexate 20mg weekly + Folic acid
current_medication.known_issues: Liver enzyme monitoring every 2 months; cannot drink alcohol; persistent fatigue day after injection; mouth sores

promotion.how_it_works: Blocks IL-17A, a specific inflammatory protein that drives both joint inflammation and skin plaques in psoriatic arthritis
advantages_over_current: Treats both joint pain AND skin symptoms simultaneously
No liver toxicity — no alcohol restrictions, no liver monitoring
Self-injection every 4 weeks after loading doses
No next-day fatigue or mouth sores
Rapid improvement — many patients see results within 3-4 weeks
potential_downsides: Increased risk of infections, especially fungal infections
Self-injection required monthly
May worsen inflammatory bowel disease if present
Higher cost without savings program
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $0 per month with the Cosentyx Copay Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with active psoriatic arthritis who have had inadequate response to DMARDs

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, tired of methotrexate restrictions
call.agent_notes: Patient's quality of life significantly impacted by methotrexate's restrictions and side effects. Cosentyx offers freedom from monitoring and alcohol restrictions.

current_medication.name: Humira (adalimumab) 40mg every 2 weeks
current_medication.known_issues: Losing effectiveness after 3 years (secondary failure); increasing joint stiffness and flares; injection site reactions worsening

promotion.how_it_works: Blocks the IL-6 receptor, targeting a different inflammatory pathway than TNF inhibitors, which is why it works when TNF inhibitors lose effectiveness
advantages_over_current: Different mechanism — works when TNF inhibitors like Humira stop working
Injection every 2 weeks — same schedule patient is already used to
Significant improvement in morning stiffness and fatigue
Can be used alone without methotrexate if needed
Lower rate of injection site reactions compared to Humira
potential_downsides: Risk of infections and low white blood cell counts — requires monitoring
Can increase cholesterol levels
May cause liver enzyme elevations
Cannot be combined with other biologics
promotion.benefit_description: Eligible patients may pay as little as $0 per month with the Kevzara Copay Card
promotion.eligibility_criteria: Commercially insured patients with moderate-to-severe RA who have had inadequate response to one or more DMARDs or TNF inhibitors

call.scenario_label: Patient answers, current biologic losing effectiveness
call.agent_notes: Patient is experiencing secondary failure on Humira — classic indication for switching to a different biologic mechanism. IL-6 pathway is a logical next step.
